## V2-only operations

- `GET /v2/client/workspaces/{workspace_id}/ai-agent/tasks/assigned-agent-profiles` (`listWorkspaceAssignedAgentProfilesV2`)
- `POST /v2/client/workspaces/{workspace_id}/ai-agent/tasks/{task_id}/agent-assignments` (`createAIAgentTaskAgentAssignmentV2`)
- `DELETE /v2/client/workspaces/{workspace_id}/ai-agent/tasks/{task_id}/agent-assignments/{agent_id}` (`deleteAIAgentTaskAgentAssignmentV2`)
- `POST /v2/client/workspaces/{workspace_id}/ai-agent/tasks/{task_id}/agent-assignments/{agent_id}/stop` (`stopAIAgentTaskAgentAssignmentV2`)
- `GET /v2/client/workspaces/{workspace_id}/ai-agent/tasks/{task_id}/thread-stream-subscription` (`getAIAgentTaskThreadStreamSubscriptionV2`)

## ClientStreamEvent variants

- `device_runtime_snapshot`
- `device_daemon_status_changed`
- `agent_editability_changed`
- `agent_work_status_changed`
- `agent_thread_progress`

## Codegen rules

- `Control-plane API enum values and sum-type variants are contract values, not free text.`
- `Domain DSL is the authoring surface, API IR preserves it, and OpenAPI is the generated client projection.`
- `Generated client code is not owned by this repository.`

## Invariant anchors

- `DSL and IR operation tuples must match exactly.`
- `OpenAPI operation tuples must match the IR projection exactly.`
- `Every v1 operation must have an equivalent workspace-scoped v2 operation.`
- `v2 may add workspace-specific task assignment/read-model operations beyond v1 compatibility.`

## Evidence Loop

- Observe: The AI Agent policy manual route list was narrower than the current DSL/IR/OpenAPI fixture surface.
- Hypothesis: A fixture-backed verifier can make API surface drift observable and keep prose from becoming a second source of truth.
- Execute: Compare DSL, IR, and OpenAPI operation tuples, count v1/v2 surfaces, verify v2-only operations, and verify ClientStreamEvent variants.
- Evaluate: CI fails when route count, route tuple parity, stream variants, generated reader, or workflow artifact binding drift.
- Retrospective: Future AI Agent endpoints should update the DSL/IR/OpenAPI fixture and this manifest before reader text changes.
